 {"id":"bd-eyto","title":"Time-dependent tests may be flaky near TTL boundary","description":"Several tombstone merge tests use time.Now() to create test data: time.Now().Add(-24 * time.Hour), time.Now().Add(-60 * 24 * time.Hour), etc. While these work reliably in practice (24h vs 30d TTL has large margin), they could theoretically be flaky if: 1) Tests run slowly, 2) System clock changes during test, 3) TTL constants change. Recommendation: Consider using a fixed reference time or time injection for deterministic tests. Lower priority since current margin is large. Files: internal/merge/merge_test.go:1337-1338, 1352-1353, 1548-1549, 1590-1591","status":"hooked","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-05T16:37:02.348143-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T15:44:43.36321-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-f0wm","title":"Swarm status doesn't update after task completions","description":"During swarm bd-784c, the swarm status remained at '9% (1/11 tasks merged)' even after 5 tasks were completed and closed.

**Observed:**
```
gt swarm status bd-784c
Progress: 9% (1/11 tasks merged)
```

But bd list showed 4+ issues closed:
- bd-j3dj, bd-kkka, bd-1tkd, bd-9btu all closed

**Expected:**
Swarm status should reflect actual completion count, either by:
1. Polling issue status from beads
2. Updating when polecats close issues
3. Updating when commits are pushed to integration branch

**Impact:**
Coordinator (me) had to manually check bd list to know actual progress.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-28T16:17:49.27816-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/dave","updated_at":"2025-12-28T22:14:20.098792-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-28T22:14:20.098792-08:00"}
